meta.title: Yar universal feature hierarchy
meta.version: v6-hierarchy-2026-07-19 (+F65 guardian, F66 ask/summarize, F67 long-memory, F68 sync, F69 diarization)
meta.source_of_truth: docs/03-Products/Cytonome/Yar/research/yar-unified-feature-comparison-v4.md (62 scored features) + 2 approved additions (F63, F64) on 2026-07-18
meta.gated_note: gated=true means safety-gated: cannot ship until crisis-detection module + privacy-boundary schema exist and are reviewed

children.name: Voice brain dump
children.desc: Talk out everything on your mind, captured for you, no typing.

children.name: Stray thought capture
children.desc: Drop a random thought or to-do somewhere safe mid-task.

children.name: Capture from anywhere
children.desc: Save to Yar from phone, computer, or browser in one tap.

children.name: Meeting-mode diarization
children.desc: Separate speakers in a conversation to build contextual meeting notes; consent-gated.

children.name: Brain dump to action plan
children.desc: Turn a voice/text dump into actual next steps automatically.

children.name: Tasks from your words
children.desc: Pull out to-dos directly from what you said, no manual sorting.

children.name: Right-sized task breakdown
children.desc: Choose how small tasks are chopped so each step feels doable.

children.name: Focus companion & body doubling
children.desc: A quiet presence with you while you work.

children.name: Single-task focus mode
children.desc: Fill the screen with one thing only.

children.name: Graceful activity pause
children.desc: Yar holds your place when you step away, no fuss.

children.name: Gentle break prompts
children.desc: A soft nudge to rest before you have been deep too long.

children.name: Floating task reminder
children.desc: A small current-task reminder in a screen corner.

children.name: Focus & adherence guardian
children.desc: With your permission across chosen apps, nudges you back to the agreed plan when you drift, at an authority level you set from gentle reminder to blocking distractions.

children.name: Flexible plan with a backup track
children.desc: A dual-track plan: what you want, plus a lighter version.

children.name: AI morning plan
children.desc: A suggested day plan around what you have on and how you feel.

children.name: Personal compass (gentle goals)
children.desc: Non-pressuring direction: gentle goals you steer by, never a performance bar.

children.name: Open data connections (MCP)
children.desc: Connect Yar to your other tools so info flows between them.

children.name: All-in-one ND support app
children.desc: Tasks, time, notes, mood, AI in one place, no app-switching.

children.name: Your energy & mood map
children.desc: A simple picture of how energy and mood are moving.

children.name: Mood tag on tasks
children.desc: Add a quick mood note to any task.

children.name: Voice wellbeing signals (research)
children.desc: Opt-in, research-stage voice-pattern signals, full consent.

children.name: Mood-anchored morning check-in
children.desc: A brief low-pressure check-in that sets the day's tone.

children.name: Voice mood awareness
children.desc: Opt-in mood signals in your voice; off by default.

children.name: Companion style & voice
children.desc: Choose the voice and presence you want from Yar.

children.name: Companion that learns your style
children.desc: Yar adjusts how it talks to match what helps you.

children.name: Mood-matched companion
children.desc: Yar shifts tone to match how you feel right now.

children.name: Adaptive companion that builds trust
children.desc: Yar reads between the lines more as trust grows.

children.name: Rest day support
children.desc: Holds space for a pause without treating it as failure.

children.name: Energy check before saying yes
children.desc: Pause to check capacity before committing to something new.

children.name: No-penalty plan change
children.desc: Change your plan anytime without guilt or a performance hit.

children.name: Streaks that honor rest days
children.desc: Consistency record that does not punish needed stops.

children.name: Gentle reset after a hard day
children.desc: A short kind debrief to process and set it down.

children.name: Your week as a story
children.desc: A short human narrative of the week, not a list.

children.name: Private space before planning
children.desc: Write how you feel before tasks, private to you.

children.name: Safety & consent layer (CAP)
children.desc: Built-in consent and safety controls; you are in charge.

children.name: On-device private AI
children.desc: AI runs on your device; data never leaves your hands.

children.name: Pre-send tone check-in
children.desc: Optional heads-up on how a message's tone might land.

children.name: Two-way communication bridge
children.desc: Translate between ND and neurotypical styles, both ways.

children.name: Invisible-disability advocacy mode
children.desc: During hard periods, helps explain your situation, needs, and what to expect to others, on your terms.

children.name: Co-planning with a trusted person
children.desc: Share planning with a trusted person, on your terms.

children.name: Social connections & your mood
children.desc: See how social interactions connect to how you feel.

children.name: Read-aloud with highlighting
children.desc: Text read aloud with words highlighted in sync.

children.name: Gentle context change cues
children.desc: A soft signal before shifting from one thing to another.

children.name: Voice-grown thought map
children.desc: Talk and watch words build into a connected visual map.

children.name: Thought placement assistant
children.desc: Suggests where a new idea fits in your existing map.

children.name: Spatial thought map view
children.desc: See notes and ideas laid out spatially with connections.

children.name: Thought map reviewer
children.desc: A gentle review catching loose threads after a session.

children.name: Untangling parallel thoughts
children.desc: Sort several simultaneous ideas into workable threads.

children.name: Conversational thought map
children.desc: Build a map through back-and-forth conversation.

children.name: Structured note types
children.desc: Flexible note types that match how your memory works.

children.name: Saved smart searches
children.desc: Active searches that surface the right notes as things change.

children.name: Your personal vocabulary
children.desc: Yar learns your names, phrases, and shorthand.

children.name: Names & terms you use
children.desc: Recognizes your people, places, and terms over time.

children.name: Map to document transform
children.desc: Turn a messy thought map into a clean document in one step.

children.name: Highlight & link on any page
children.desc: Highlight anything on any web page and link it into notes.

children.name: Thought to document templates
children.desc: Apply a template to a map to draft a doc, report, or email.

children.name: Ask & summarize your captures
children.desc: Ask questions across your captures and get grounded summaries from your own knowledge.

children.name: Your data in your own tools
children.desc: Use Yar alongside Obsidian, Logseq; data stays yours.

children.name: Open schema translation layer
children.desc: Move notes/data between apps and formats without losing structure.

children.name: Private local knowledge store
children.desc: Notes live on your device in an open format you own.

children.name: Long-term personal memory
children.desc: A personal, temporal memory graph so Yar recalls people, terms, and threads across sessions.

children.name: Cross-device sync
children.desc: Keep your data consistent across phone and laptop, local-first and under your control.

children.name: Open sensor connection layer (CSP)
children.desc: A flexible layer to connect data sources and devices you choose.

children.name: Wearable sensor connection
children.desc: Connect an Oura ring or Apple Watch; factor in body data.

children.name: Brain sensor connection layer
children.desc: Future, opt-in connection for non-invasive brain sensing.

children.name: Your custom tracking axes
children.desc: Define your own dimensions to track that no app covers.

children.name: Opt-in self-assessment tools
children.desc: Validated optional self-assessments, never diagnostic.

children.name: Personalized gentle nudges
children.desc: Nudges tuned to your patterns, arriving when useful.

children.name: Layered wellbeing dashboard
children.desc: Wellbeing data in layers you open as much as you want.
